skill.name: segment-automation
skill.description: Use Rube MCP to operate Segment workflows, including tracking, identify, group, page, alias, batch, and source management tasks.
skill.summary: Automate common Segment customer data workflows through Rube MCP with schema-first tool discovery.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and ordinary text, not executable code. The real risk is medium because the skill routes customer analytics data through Rube MCP to Segment and includes source update guidance that can affect data collection.

medium_findings.title: Third-party MCP Handles Segment Customer Data
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and an active Segment connection, then guides calls that can send identifiers, traits, context, events, page views, groups, aliases, and batches. This is legitimate for the skill purpose but creates privacy and data-routing risk if users include sensitive data or connect the wrong workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly depends on Rube MCP and describes Segment calls containing user identifiers and traits. The behavior is expected automation, but the data sensitivity and external service path are clear.

medium_findings.title: Source Configuration Changes Can Affect Collection
medium_findings.description: The skill includes optional source schema and source update operations. Updating a Segment source can change how data is collected or validated, so these operations need workspace review and permission controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source update capability is directly listed, and the skill warns that source updates may affect data collection. No malicious intent is shown, but operational impact is plausible.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code spans around MCP tool names, parameters, and examples. No executable script, shell command, Ruby code, or command interpolation was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed contexts show documentation formatting only. There are no code files or runnable shell snippets in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography lines do not contain cryptographic operations. They are ordinary Segment guidance about descriptions, event usage, privacy, destination support, message fields, PII, and response parsing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no hashing, encryption, randomness, or cryptographic API usage. The static detector likely matched short text tokens without semantic context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded MCP Endpoint Is a Documented External Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill names the Rube MCP endpoint in setup instructions. This is not covert exfiltration, but it confirms that users must trust an external MCP service before using the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicit setup documentation and aligns with the declared Rube MCP dependency. The remaining concern is dependency trust, not hidden network behavior.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines reference Segment data quality, message validation, and PII cautions. No local system probing, environment discovery, host inspection, or credential harvesting instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal analytics documentation. They do not instruct the model to inspect the host system or collect local machine details.

content.value_statement: Segment operations can be slow and error-prone when schemas, identities, and batches are handled manually. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for consistent Segment automation.
actual_capabilities: Searches Rube MCP tools first so Segment schemas are current before actions are planned.
Guides Segment connection checks through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS before workflows run.
Documents track, identify, group, page, alias, and batch workflows with required fields.
Explains identity resolution order for anonymous users, signed-up users, and group membership.
Provides naming, timestamp, rate limit, and response parsing guidance for Segment calls.
Includes optional source schema review and source update workflows.
limitations: Requires a working Rube MCP server and an authenticated Segment connection.
Does not validate live Segment destination behavior after an accepted API response.
Does not prevent users from sending sensitive data in traits, context, or properties.
Source update actions should be reviewed by a Segment administrator before execution.

use_cases.title: Instrument Product Events
use_cases.description: Plan and send consistent track events with required identifiers, event names, properties, and timestamps.

use_cases.title: Maintain User Profiles
use_cases.description: Identify users, merge anonymous identities, and connect users to organizations with the correct Segment call order.

use_cases.title: Run Bulk Analytics Imports
use_cases.description: Prepare batch messages for historical imports or high-volume event delivery while respecting Segment limits.

prompt_templates.title: Check Segment Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to verify that Rube MCP is available, search for current Segment tools, and confirm my Segment connection status before any action.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Track Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare a Segment track call for a user action. Confirm the required identifier, event name, properties, and timestamp format first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Identity Resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design the Segment call sequence for an anonymous visitor who signs up, receives traits, completes an action, and joins an organization.

prompt_templates.title: Review Batch and Source Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to inspect current Segment tool schemas, plan a batch import, and flag any source configuration changes that need administrator approval.

output_examples.input: Prepare tracking for a completed order event.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the Segment connection, checks the current track schema, asks for the user identifier, event name, order properties, and timestamp, then prepares the call plan.

output_examples.input: Merge anonymous activity after signup and update the user profile.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends an alias call from the anonymous ID to the user ID, followed by identify and track calls with consistent traits and event properties.

output_examples.input: Import a list of historical page views and purchases.
output_examples.output: The assistant groups records into batch messages, checks required fields for each message type, and highlights timestamp and rate limit considerations.

best_practices: Search current Rube tool schemas before every workflow because MCP tool definitions can change.
Use consistent event, property, and trait names before sending data to downstream destinations.
Review PII, destination settings, and source changes before running identify, batch, or update actions.
anti_patterns: Do not send Segment calls before confirming the Rube MCP connection is active.
Do not use alias repeatedly for the same identity merge because Segment treats it as one-way.
Do not treat accepted API responses as proof that every downstream destination received the data.

faq.question: Does this skill require Segment API keys?
faq.answer: The skill expects authentication through Rube MCP. Users still need an active Segment connection in Rube.

faq.question: Can it send track and identify calls?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for track, identify, group, page, alias, and batch Segment calls.

faq.question: Does it verify current tool schemas?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to search Rube tools first before using Segment operations.

faq.question: Can it handle bulk imports?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains Segment batch operations and the need to check limits and per-message requirements.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive customer data?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill warns against sending sensitive PII unless Segment destinations are configured for it.

faq.question: Can it change Segment source settings?
faq.answer: It includes source update guidance, but these changes should be reviewed by a Segment administrator.
